description: A key architectural deliverable produced by the Architecture Design function to map system boundaries, external entities, and high-level interactions.

name: Data Flow Diagrams
description: A technical documentation artifact detailing how data moves through the bank's systems, managed under the Architecture Design function.

name: Technology Standards
description: Formal technical guidelines and mandatory specifications that govern system design, development, and integration across the bank's technology landscape.

name: API Design
description: The architectural process and deliverables for designing application programming interfaces, ensuring standardized integration across bank systems.

name: Integration Management
description: A governance and operational domain overseeing the design, implementation, and maintenance of cross-system connectivity and data flow within FirstUK Bank's architecture.

name: Integration Patterns
description: Standardized architectural approaches and methodologies used to connect disparate systems and ensure consistent data exchange across FirstUK Bank's technology landscape.

name: Data Integration Pipelines
description: Automated data processing workflows responsible for extracting, transforming, and loading information between FirstUK Bank's internal systems and external platforms.

name: System-to-System Interfaces
description: Technical endpoints and communication protocols enabling direct machine-readable interactions between FirstUK Bank's core applications, third-party services, and internal modules.

name: Interoperability Standards
description: Formal technical and operational specifications that govern how FirstUK Bank's systems must interact, ensuring consistent data formats, security protocols, and reliable cross-platform communication.

name: Application Landscape
description: A comprehensive architectural document mapping the bank's application portfolio, providing a high-level view of system interdependencies, functional coverage, and lifecycle status across the enterprise technology stack.

name: Context Diagram
description: A boundary-focused architectural artifact illustrating the system's external interactions, including actors, external systems, and data flow boundaries within the FirstUK Bank ecosystem.

name: Logical Data Model
description: A conceptual data architecture specification defining the logical structure, entities, attributes, and relationships of the bank's data assets independent of physical implementation or database technology.

name: API Catalogue
description: A centralized registry documenting the bank's application programming interfaces, including endpoints, specifications, versioning, and governance metadata for internal and external consumption.

name: Integration Specification
description: A technical design document detailing the protocols, data formats, security requirements, and error handling mechanisms governing system-to-system communications within the bank's architecture.

name: Technology Department
description: A cross-functional organizational unit responsible for providing technical governance across the bank's product development and system implementation processes.

name: Product Management Department
description: A cross-functional organizational unit tasked with evaluating feature feasibility to ensure new banking products align with strategic and operational requirements.

name: Operations Department
description: A cross-functional organizational unit that conducts system design reviews to validate operational viability and ensure alignment with bank infrastructure standards.

name: Risk & Compliance Department
description: A cross-functional organizational unit responsible for performing architecture audits to assess regulatory compliance, risk exposure, and control effectiveness across bank systems.

name: Marketing Department
description: The Marketing Department is a core organizational unit responsible for promotional and strategic marketing activities within FirstUK Bank. It reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer and operates from the Head Office location. The department maintains a fixed operational headcount of one staff member as defined in the structural documentation.

name: Head of Marketing
description: This role serves as the designated leadership position responsible for directing the Marketing Department's operations and strategic initiatives. It carries defined accountability within the bank's reporting structure, answering directly to the CEO.

name: Chief Executive Officer
description: The Chief Executive Officer represents the top executive leadership tier within FirstUK Bank's governance framework. This role holds ultimate decision authority and serves as the direct reporting line for the Marketing Department.

name: Marketing Department Headcount
description: This metric specifies the operational staffing allocation for the Marketing Department, indicating a single employee capacity. It functions as a structural headcount target defining the department's resource limits and organizational footprint.

description: The Head Office serves as the primary physical location where the Marketing Department is based and conducts its daily operations. It represents the central administrative site designated for this organizational unit within FirstUK Bank.

name: Customer
description: A core banking ontology entity representing the target audience for brand awareness and acquisition initiatives outlined in the organization's strategic mission.

description: A core banking ontology entity representing the financial offerings that require launch support as part of the bank's strategic mission.

name: Brand & Communications
description: Internal department responsible for managing the bank's brand identity, messaging strategy, website content, social media presence, and public relations and media relations activities.

description: A core business unit or strategic focus area responsible for driving new customer growth. It encompasses the management of acquisition campaigns, channel optimization, partner marketing initiatives, and conversion optimization processes.

description: Targeted marketing initiatives and programs designed to attract, engage, and onboard new customers into the bank's ecosystem.

description: The strategic process of refining and improving customer-facing channels to enhance engagement, usability, and acquisition efficiency.

description: The systematic improvement of customer journey touchpoints and processes to increase the rate at which prospects become active customers.

description: A dedicated functional support area responsible for orchestrating new product rollouts. It oversees the creation of marketing collateral, equips sales teams with necessary tools, and manages direct customer communications throughout the launch lifecycle.

name: Legal Department

name: Legal & Regulatory Affairs Department
description: Defines the core mandate of the department responsible for providing legal counsel and managing regulatory affairs across FirstUK Bank.
context: Provide legal counsel and manage regulatory affairs.

name: Contract Management
description: A functional business unit or department responsible for governing contractual relationships and documentation across the bank. It oversees the development of Terms & Conditions (T&Cs), conducts legal document reviews, and manages both vendor contracts and partner agreements to ensure regulatory compliance and operational alignment.

name: Regulatory Affairs
description: Internal department responsible for managing ongoing regulatory engagement, tracking incoming regulatory circulars, providing support for regulatory responses, and overseeing legal compliance functions across the bank.

name: Financial Conduct Authority
description: UK financial regulatory authority with which the bank maintains active engagement and from which it receives regulatory guidance and circulars.

name: Prudential Regulation Authority
description: UK financial regulatory authority focused on prudential standards, engaged by the bank as part of its broader regulatory compliance and response framework.

name: Litigation & Dispute Resolution
description: A dedicated functional area within FirstUK Bank responsible for overseeing formal legal proceedings, managing escalated customer complaints, and coordinating the end-to-end resolution of disputes. This unit serves as the central governance point for legal risk mitigation, ensuring all claims are processed in compliance with regulatory standards and internal control frameworks.

name: Customer Disputes
description: Represents formal disagreements or complaints raised by customers regarding banking products, services, or account handling. Within the bank's operational framework, these disputes trigger structured review pathways and escalation protocols to ensure compliant resolution and regulatory reporting.

name: Legal Claims Management
description: The structured process of tracking, assessing, and resolving formal legal claims brought against the bank. It encompasses documentation requirements, liability assessment, and strategic decision-making to align with regulatory obligations and minimize financial and reputational exposure.

name: Settlement Negotiations
description: The formalized process of reaching mutually agreeable resolutions with claimants or disputing parties outside of full litigation. This concept governs how the bank evaluates settlement terms, secures necessary approvals for financial concessions, and documents final agreements to close claims.

name: Expert Witness Coordination
description: A specialized operational function responsible for identifying, engaging, and managing external legal or technical experts required to support the bank's position in formal disputes or litigation. Ensures compliance with court procedures, maintains expert availability for high-stakes claims, and coordinates briefing materials.

name: Terms & Conditions
description: Internal legal documentation that defines the contractual terms and conditions applicable to each bank product, serving as the primary governance framework for customer agreements and product delivery.

name: Privacy Policy
description: Formal compliance document outlining the bank's data protection standards and privacy obligations, identified by internal tracking code PRI-POL-004.

name: Data Processing Agreement
description: Legal framework governing how the bank processes personal data, ensuring adherence to data protection regulations and establishing mandatory standards for third-party data handling.

name: Vendor Agreements
description: Contractual documents that establish the terms, obligations, and compliance requirements for all third-party vendor relationships, governing service delivery and risk allocation.

name: Compliance
description: Internal department responsible for regulatory coordination, ensuring the bank's activities and cross-functional interfaces align with external legal and supervisory requirements.

name: Risk & Compliance
description: Cross-functional unit tasked with conducting policy legal reviews to ensure internal governance, risk management frameworks, and contractual terms meet regulatory standards.

name: Customer Service
description: Frontline operational unit providing dispute support to resolve customer complaints, manage service escalations, and maintain client satisfaction.

name: Operations
description: Core business unit handling contract reviews to validate terms, conditions, and legal enforceability of banking agreements and operational documentation.

name: Internal Audit
description: A core assurance and governance department responsible for independent audit functions within FirstUK Bank, operating under a dedicated reporting line to the board.
context: Reports to: Board Audit Committee (independent line)

name: Head of Internal Audit
description: The named leadership role responsible for directing the Internal Audit department, with defined accountability and an independent reporting line to the Board Audit Committee.
context: Reports to: Board Audit Committee (independent line)

name: Board Audit Committee
description: A board-level governance committee overseeing audit and risk assurance activities, receiving independent oversight reports from the Internal Audit department.

name: Internal Audit Department Headcount
description: The authorized staffing level for the Internal Audit department, specified as a single full-time equivalent position.

name: Head Office
description: The primary corporate location and operational base where the Internal Audit department is physically situated.

name: Independent Assurance Mandate
description: Establishes the core governance mandate for the assurance function, requiring independent verification and evaluation of risk management frameworks and internal control effectiveness across the organization.
context: Provide independent assurance on risk management and control effectiveness.

name: Control Testing
description: A primary internal audit and governance function encompassing the evaluation of operational processes, design validity, and effectiveness of controls within FirstUK Bank.

name: Process Walkthroughs
description: A control testing activity involving step-by-step tracing of transactions or processes through system and operational controls to verify design and implementation.

name: Control Effectiveness Testing
description: A control testing activity focused on evaluating whether operational controls operate as designed and achieve their intended risk mitigation objectives.

name: Control Design Reviews
description: A control testing activity that assesses the structural design and logical framework of controls to ensure they adequately address identified risks.

name: Audit Procedures
description: Standardized operational steps and methodologies applied during control testing to gather evidence, validate processes, and assess compliance with internal standards.

name: Risk-Based Audit
description: A governance and assurance process framework that structures the bank's internal audit activities around risk prioritization. It encompasses the end-to-end lifecycle of audit planning, execution, reporting, and follow-up to ensure compliance and operational effectiveness across FirstUK Bank.

name: Annual Audit Plan
description: A yearly strategic roadmap that outlines the scope, frequency, and risk-prioritized focus areas for the bank's internal audit activities. It serves as the foundational document guiding resource allocation and audit coverage across the organization.

name: Audit Execution
description: The operational phase of the audit lifecycle where planned procedures are carried out to assess controls, verify compliance, and evaluate risk management effectiveness within FirstUK Bank.

name: Finding and Observation Reporting
description: The formal documentation and communication mechanism used to record audit results, control gaps, and operational observations identified during the execution phase. It ensures transparency and provides actionable insights for governance bodies.

name: Management Response Tracking
description: A continuous monitoring process that follows up on management's corrective actions and remediation plans in response to audit findings. It ensures accountability and verifies that identified risks are effectively mitigated over time.

name: Audit Committee
description: An internal governance body responsible for overseeing audit activities and receiving formal reporting on findings. The committee serves as the primary recipient of structured audit deliverables to ensure oversight and accountability.

name: Audit Reports
description: Formal documentation generated to summarize audit activities, findings, and compliance status. These reports are produced on a defined schedule to provide stakeholders with structured insights into operational and control effectiveness.

name: Quarterly Reporting Cadence
description: A defined operational schedule dictating that audit reporting deliverables must be generated and reviewed every three months. This cadence ensures timely oversight and aligns with standard governance cycles.

name: Finding Tracking Process
description: An operational control requiring the systematic logging, monitoring, and remediation of audit findings. This rule ensures that identified issues are tracked to closure and do not remain unaddressed.

name: Management Letter
description: A formal communication document issued to senior management detailing significant audit observations, recommendations, or control deficiencies. It serves as a key deliverable in the post-audit reporting phase.

name: KYC Verification
description: A core banking concept representing the Know Your Customer verification process, tracked as an annual audit area within FirstUK Bank's Annual Audit Plan. The audit status for this domain was completed in Q4 2025.

name: AML Screening
description: A core banking concept representing Anti-Money Laundering screening procedures, grouped with account opening processes for annual audit review. The audit status for this domain was completed in Q4 2025.

name: Payment Processing System
description: A technology system or platform responsible for handling customer payment transactions, subject to annual audit review under FirstUK Bank's Annual Audit Plan. The audit is currently in progress for Q1 2026.

name: Fraud Detection Rules
description: Operational constraints and decision logic governing fraud detection mechanisms, scheduled for bi-annual audit review. The next scheduled audit is set for Q2 2026.

name: IT General Controls
description: Foundational technology and infrastructure controls ensuring system integrity, security, and compliance, subject to annual audit review. The audit for this area was completed in Q1 2026.

name: Operational Risk Management
description: A core banking domain addressing risks arising from inadequate or failed internal processes, people, and systems. Scheduled for annual audit review in Q3 2026.

name: Risk & Compliance
description: A cross-functional department responsible for coordinating audit activities across the bank. It serves as the primary liaison for aligning risk management functions with internal audit requirements to ensure regulatory and operational compliance.

name: Board Audit Committee
description: A governance committee operating at the board level, tasked with receiving audit reports and overseeing internal control effectiveness. It acts as the final reporting destination for audit findings to ensure board-level oversight.

name: All Departments
description: A collective designation encompassing every operational division within the bank. This group is designated to provide logistical, data, and personnel support during audit fieldwork phases to facilitate comprehensive testing and verification.

name: Executive Committee
description: A key cross-departmental governance body that meets weekly to oversee and make strategic decisions for FirstUK Bank. This committee serves as a central decision-making forum for high-level organisational direction and executive oversight.

name: Product Committee
description: A specialized governance committee responsible for product oversight and governance, operating on a bi-weekly meeting cadence. It aligns cross-functional teams to ensure product development and lifecycle management adhere to bank standards.

name: Compliance Committee
description: A regulatory-focused governance body that convenes monthly to track and implement regulatory updates across the organisation. It ensures operational practices remain aligned with external compliance requirements and internal policy frameworks.

name: Risk Committee
description: An enterprise-level oversight committee that meets quarterly to monitor and manage the bank's overall risk exposure. It provides strategic direction on risk appetite, controls, and governance frameworks across all business units.

name: Executive Committee
description: The top-tier governance body in FirstUK Bank's escalation framework, serving as the final decision authority for issues that cannot be resolved at lower management levels.
context: Escalation Path: Issue → Manager → Director → Executive Committee

name: Manager
description: The first line of leadership accountability in the bank's escalation framework, responsible for initial issue review and resolution before escalation to senior directors.
context: Escalation Path: Issue → Manager → Director → Executive Committee

name: Director
description: A senior leadership role positioned between management and the executive committee, acting as an intermediate decision authority for escalated issues.
context: Escalation Path: Issue → Manager → Director → Executive Committee

name: Escalation Matrix
description: A formalized operational framework (documented as ESC-001) that defines the mandatory escalation path and decision authority structure for handling issues within FirstUK Bank.
context: Escalation Path: Issue → Manager → Director → Executive Committee

name: Retail Banking
description: A core retail banking department responsible for customer-facing financial services, tracked against Net Promoter Score performance targets.

name: Product Management
description: A department overseeing product development and feature lifecycle, measured against quarterly delivery output targets.

name: Operations
description: An operational unit managing account processing and onboarding workflows, evaluated via service level agreement compliance.

name: Customer Service
description: A frontline support department focused on client assistance, measured on initial contact effectiveness and resolution rates.

name: Financial Crime
description: A specialized compliance and risk unit managing financial crime reporting, specifically tracking the timeliness of Suspicious Activity Reports.
context: Financial Crime | SAR Timeliness | <30 days | 20 days

name: Compliance
description: A governance and regulatory adherence department responsible for policy oversight, review cycles, and internal standard enforcement.

name: Risk
description: A risk management department overseeing capital adequacy, financial stability thresholds, and regulatory capital ratios.

name: Technology
description: An IT and infrastructure department responsible for maintaining system uptime, technical availability standards, and platform reliability.

name: NPS Target
description: A customer loyalty performance metric targeting a Net Promoter Score of 70, with an actual recorded performance of 72.

name: New Feature Delivery Target
description: A product development output metric targeting the delivery of 4 new features per quarter, with an actual rate of 3 per quarter.

name: Account Opening SLA Target
description: An operational service level agreement targeting a 95% completion rate for account openings, achieving an actual performance of 98%.

name: First-Call Resolution Target
description: A customer support effectiveness metric targeting an 85% first-contact resolution rate, with an actual achievement of 81%.

name: SAR Timeliness Target
description: A financial crime reporting threshold targeting Suspicious Activity Report submission within 30 days or less, with an actual average of 20 days.
context: Financial Crime | SAR Timeliness | <30 days | 20 days

name: Policy Review Timeliness Target
description: A compliance oversight metric targeting a 100% on-time policy review completion rate, achieving exactly 100%.

name: Capital Ratio Target
description: A financial stability metric targeting a capital adequacy ratio greater than 15%, with an actual recorded ratio of 18.2%.

name: System Availability Target
description: A technology infrastructure metric targeting 99.8% system uptime, achieving an actual performance of 99.85%.

name: Organisation Model
description: Internal reference document or framework defining FirstUK Bank's organizational structure, reporting lines, and governance hierarchy.

name: Systems
description: Central repository or architectural framework detailing FirstUK Bank's technology systems and their integration touchpoints across the enterprise.

name: Escalation Matrix
description: Governance document establishing escalation procedures, decision thresholds, and operational limits for resolving issues within FirstUK Bank.

name: Regulatory Circulars
description: Collection of regulatory guidance and mandatory directives issued to FirstUK Bank for the 2026 period, governing compliance and operational conduct.

name: Compliance Bulletins
description: Internal or external compliance updates and directives governing FirstUK Bank's adherence standards, risk controls, and policy updates for 2026.

name: Chief Operating Officer
description: A C-suite leadership role designated as an approver for the document, indicating executive-level accountability and sign-off authority within FirstUK Bank's governance process.

name: Chief Executive Officer
description: A C-suite leadership role designated as an approver for the document, representing the highest executive authority and final sign-off accountability within FirstUK Bank's governance process.
